title: Verilog parameter propagation does not work
body:
Parameter propagation does not seem to be complete. The "OPERATION_WIDTH" parameter value from the "ALU_Test_Top" module seems to not be propagated to the "output wire [OPERATION_WIDTH - 1:0] o_result" line.

Setting the default value of the "OPERATION_WIDTH" parameter in the "ALU_Test" module to 32 (i.e. replacing "#(parameter OPERATION_WIDTH = 0)" with "#(parameter OPERATION_WIDTH = 32)") makes the warning go away.

The above indicates that the parameter value is not propagated to the line "output wire [OPERATION_WIDTH - 1:0] o_result" and the default value is used instead.

**Yosys Version**: 06104249406972de01d0360df63a32cafcdf2ec5

## Steps to reproduce the issue

Run the following command: yosys -f "verilog -sv" -p "synth_ice40 -top ALU_Test_Top -json ALU_Test_Top.json" ALU_Test.v ALU_Test_Top.v

with the below code:

**ALU_Test_Top.v**
```
`default_nettype none   // Don't allow undeclared nets
module ALU_Test_Top
(
    output wire [31:0] o_result,
    output wire o_carry
);
    ALU_Test #(.OPERATION_WIDTH(32)) alu //Using 32'd32 produces the same behavior
    (
        .i_operand0(32'd1),
        .i_operand1(32'd1),
        .o_result(o_result),
        .o_carry(o_carry)
    );
endmodule
```
**ALU_Test.v**
```
`default_nettype none   // Don't allow undeclared nets
module ALU_Test
#(parameter OPERATION_WIDTH = 0)
(
    input wire [OPERATION_WIDTH - 1:0] i_operand0,
    inout wire [OPERATION_WIDTH - 1:0] i_operand1,
    output wire [OPERATION_WIDTH - 1:0] o_result,
    output wire o_carry
);
    assign o_result = i_operand0 + i_operand1;
    assign o_carry = o_result[31];
endmodule
```


## Expected behavior

No warnings

## Actual behavior

Getting a warning: "**Warning**: Range select out of bounds on signal `\o_result': Setting result bit to undef."
code_blocks:
`default_nettype none   // Don't allow undeclared nets
module ALU_Test_Top
(
    output wire [31:0] o_result,
    output wire o_carry
);
    ALU_Test #(.OPERATION_WIDTH(32)) alu //Using 32'd32 produces the same behavior
    (
        .i_operand0(32'd1),
        .i_operand1(32'd1),
        .o_result(o_result),
        .o_carry(o_carry)
    );
endmodule
